blocks:
سُورة الغاشية

مكيّة عدد آيها ستّ وعشرون آية بلا خلاف بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) هَلْ أَتاكَ حَدِيثُ الْغاشِيَةِ ** (1) Has the story of the Overwhelming Event come to you? The calamity (الداهية) that overwhelms (تغشى) people with its severities (بشدائدها), meaning the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة).

(٢) وُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ خاشِعَةٌ ** (2) Faces on that day will be humbled, Abased (ذليلة).

(٣) عامِلَةٌ ناصِبَةٌ ** (3) Toiling, weary, They toiled (عملت) and became weary (ونصبت) in deeds that will not benefit them on that day.

(٤) تَصْلى ناراً حامِيَةً ** (4) Entering a scorching fire, Extreme (متناهية) in heat.

(٥) تُسْقى مِنْ عَيْنٍ آنِيَةٍ ** (5) Given to drink from a boiling spring. It has reached its utmost limit (إناها) in heat.

Al-Qummi said: They are those who opposed the religion of Allah. And they prayed (وصلّوا) and fasted (وصاموا) and showed enmity (ونصبوا) towards the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). They worked (عملوا) and became weary (ونصبوا), yet nothing of their deeds will be accepted from them. And their faces تَصْلى ** (will enter) ناراً حامِيَةً.** (a scorching fire).

(٦) لَيْسَ لَهُمْ طَعامٌ إِلَّا مِنْ ضَرِيعٍ. (6) They will have no food except ḍarīʿ, (٧) لا يُسْمِنُ وَلا يُغْنِي مِنْ جُوعٍ ** (7) which neither nourishes nor avails against hunger. He said: It is the sweat of the inhabitants of the Fire and what comes out of the private parts of the adulteresses (الزّواني). In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: The Ḍarīʿ (الضّريع) is something that exists in the Fire, resembling thorns, more bitter than aloes (الصبر), fouler than a carcass (الجيفة), and hotter than the Fire. Allah named it al-Ḍarīʿ (الضّريع). And in the narration of al-Qummi, narrated from him صلى الله عليه وآله, from Gabriel (جبرائيل): If a single drop of the Ḍarīʿ (الضّريع) were to fall into the drink of the people of the world, the people of the world would die from its foulness (نتنها). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: The Nasib (الناصب) does not care whether he prays or commits adultery, and this Ayah was revealed concerning them: عامِلَةٌ ناصِبَةٌ تَصْلى ناراً حامِيَةً. (3) toiling, weary, (4) entering a scorching fire. And narrated from him (al-Sadiq عليه السلام), from his father, from the Commander of the Faithful عليهم السلام, he said: Every Nasib (ناصب), even if he engages in worship (تعبّد) and strives (اجتهد), is attributed to this Ayah: عامِلَةٌ ناصِبَةٌ. (3) toiling, weary, And in al-Majalis and al-Majma', narrated from him عليه السلام: The like of it (is narrated). And in the narration of al-Qummi: "Everyone who opposes you (خالفكم), even if he engages in worship (تعبّد) and strives (اجتهد)..." (and the rest of) the narration (الحديث). And in al-Kafi, narrated from him عليه السلام, regarding the Almighty's saying: هَلْ أَتاكَ حَدِيثُ الْغاشِيَةِ ** (Has the story of the Enveloper reached you?). He said: al-Qa'im (القائم) عليه السلام will envelop them with the sword. خاشِعَةٌ ** (humbled). He said: They will not be able to resist (الامتناع). عامِلَةٌ ** (toiling). He said: They acted with other than what Allah revealed. ناصِبَةٌ ** (weary). He said: They set up (نصبت) others besides the guardians of Allah's command (ولاة أمر الله). تَصْلى ناراً حامِيَةً ** (entering a scorching fire). He said: They will enter the fire of war in this world against the people of al-Qa'im عليه السلام, and in the Hereafter, the Fire of Hell (نار جهنّم). And in a narration: al-Ghashiyah (الْغاشِيَةِ ) are those who approach (يغشون) the Imam عليه السلام. ** لا يُسْمِنُ وَلا يُغْنِي مِنْ جُوعٍ ** (which neither nourishes nor avails against hunger). He said: Their entering (الدّخول) will not benefit them, nor will their sitting (القعود) avail them.

(٨) وُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ ناعِمَةٌ Possessing splendor (ذات بهجة). Al-Qummi said: They are the followers of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). (8) [Some] faces on that day will be blissful, (٩) لِسَعْيِها راضِيَةٌ He said: Allah will be pleased with what they strove for. (9) pleased with their striving, (١٠) فِي جَنَّةٍ عالِيَةٍ. (10) in a lofty Garden. (١١) لا تَسْمَعُ فِيها لاغِيَةً He said: It is jest (الهزل) and lies (الكذب). And it was recited based on the passive construction (على بناء المفعول) with the letter tā (بالتّاء) and with the letter yā (بالياء). (11) They will not hear any vain talk in it. (١٢) فِيها عَيْنٌ جارِيَةٌ Its flow will not cease. (12) In it is a flowing spring. (١٣) فِيها سُرُرٌ مَرْفُوعَةٌ High in thickness (السّمك) and status (القدر). (13) In it are raised couches, (١٤) وَأَكْوابٌ مَوْضُوعَةٌ The kūb (الكوب) is a vessel (إناء) that has no handle (عروة). (14) and set-out goblets, (١٥) وَنَمارِقُ مَصْفُوفَةٌ Some next to others. Al-Qummi said: They are carpets (البسط) and pillows (الوسائد). (15) and cushions set in rows, (١٦) وَزَرابِيُّ مَبْثُوثَةٌ He said: Everything that Allah created in Paradise has an example (مثال) in this world, except for the zarābī (الزّرابيّ), for it is not known what they are. And it was said: The namāriq (النمارق) are cushions (المساند), and the zarābī (الزّرابيّ) are luxurious carpets (البسط الفاخرة), the plural of zarbiyyah (زربية). مَبْثُوثَةٌ ** meaning: spread out (مبسوطة). (16) and spread carpets. And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Were it not that Allah the Almighty had decreed them (قدّرها) for them, their eyes would have been dazzled (لالتمعت أبصارهم) by what they see.

(١٧)** أَفَلا يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَى الْإِبِلِ كَيْفَ خُلِقَتْ ** (17) Do they not observe the camel, how it has been created?

[It is] a creation (خلقاً) indicating the perfection of His power and the excellence of His management (تدبيره), since He created it for dragging heavy loads (الأثقال) to distant lands (البلاد النائية). So He made it great, kneeling for the load, rising up with the load, and obedient to whoever leads it. [It has] long necks so that it may bear the heavy burdens (الأوقار). It grazes on every growing thing and endures thirst, so that it becomes possible for it to traverse the deserts (البراري) and wildernesses (المفاوز). The Almighty Allah said: **وَتَحْمِلُ أَثْقالَكُمْ إِلى بَلَدٍ لَمْ تَكُونُوا بالِغِيهِ إِلَّا بِشِقِّ الْأَنْفُسِ ** (7) And they carry your burdens to a town which you could not reach except by straining yourselves. along with the other benefits it possesses.

(١٨) وَإِلَى السَّماءِ كَيْفَ رُفِعَتْ ** (18) and to the sky, how it has been raised? without pillars (عَمَد). (١٩) وَإِلَى الْجِبالِ كَيْفَ نُصِبَتْ ** (19) and to the mountains, how they have been set up? firmly established (راسخة), they do not incline. (٢٠) وَإِلَى الْأَرْضِ كَيْفَ سُطِحَتْ ** (20) and to the earth, how it has been spread out? It was spread out (بسطت) until it became a cradle (مهاداً). In al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): He recited the opening letters of all these words with a fathah (فتح) and the tā' (التاء) with a ḍammah (ضمّ). (٢١) فَذَكِّرْ إِنَّما أَنْتَ مُذَكِّرٌ ** (21) So admonish—for you are only an admonisher; So, it is not incumbent upon you if they do not reflect or do not remember, since your duty is only the conveyance (البلاغ). (٢٢) لَسْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ بِمُصَيْطِرٍ ** (22) you are not a taskmaster over them— meaning: a dominator (بمتسلّط). And it was recited with the sīn (بالسّين). Al-Qummi said: You are neither a guardian (حافظ) nor a recorder (كاتب) over them. (٢٣) إِلَّا مَنْ تَوَلَّى وَكَفَرَ ** (23) except him who turns away and disbelieves, but [it is] he who turns away and disbelieves. (٢٤) فَيُعَذِّبُهُ اللهُ الْعَذابَ الْأَكْبَرَ ** (24) whereat Allah will punish him with the greatest punishment. the harsh (الغليظ), severe (الشّديد), perpetual (الدّائم) [punishment]. (٢٥) إِنَّ إِلَيْنا إِيابَهُمْ ** (25) Indeed, to Us is their return. their return (رجوعهم) and their ultimate destination (مصيرهم) after death. (٢٦) ثُمَّ إِنَّ عَلَيْنا حِسابَهُمْ ** (26) Then indeed, their reckoning is with Us. their recompense (جزاءهم) for their deeds. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When the Day of Resurrection occurs, and Allah gathers the first and the last for the decisive judgment (لفصل الخطاب), the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم will be called, and the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) will be called. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم will be clothed in a green robe (حلّة خضراء) that illuminates what is between the East and the West, and Ali (peace be upon him) will be clothed in one like it. And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم will be clothed in a rose-colored robe (حلّة ورديّة) that illuminates what is between the East and the West, and Ali (peace be upon him) will be clothed in one like it. Then they will ascend near Him. Then we will be called, and the reckoning (حساب) of the people will be handed over to us. By Allah, it is we who will admit the people of Paradise into Paradise and the people of the Fire into the Fire. And narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): The return (إياب) of this creation is to us, and عَلَيْنا حِسابَهُمْ ** (their reckoning is with Us). Whatever sin they committed between themselves and Allah the Almighty, we decreed upon Allah that He relinquish it to us, and He responded to us regarding that. And whatever was between them and the people, we requested it as a gift (استوهبناه) from them, and they responded to that, and Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, compensated them. In al-Amali, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: When the Day of Resurrection occurs, Allah will entrust us with the reckoning (حساب) of our followers (شيعتنا). Whatever was due to Allah, we will ask Allah to bestow it upon us, and it will be for them. And whatever was due to us, it will be for them. In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): Whoever habitually recites Hal Ataka Hadith al-Ghashiyah (هل أتاك حديث الغاشية) in an obligatory (فريضة) or supererogatory (نافلة) prayer, Allah will cover him with His mercy in this world and the Hereafter, and He will grant him security (الأمن) on the Day of Resurrection from the punishment of the Fire, if Allah the Almighty wills.
